title: Online change-point / run-length features for impending stop regimes
summary: Pit decisions often follow abrupt regime shifts rather than smooth degradation alone.
rationale: Pit decisions often follow abrupt regime shifts rather than smooth degradation alone. Online Bayesian change-point methods are designed to detect such transitions in nonstationary sequences using only past data.
implementation_hint: For each driver-stint, run a lightweight causal detector on LapTime_Delta, degradation gain, and possibly position-change residuals: posterior change probability, estimated run length, time since last change, and recent change intensity. Add these as features or as a small expert blended by rank.
expected_effect: Could surface late-stint cliff behavior and capture stop triggers that the current smooth EWMA pressure features miss.
risk: Easy to overfit if the detector is too sensitive; must verify gains under forward/event-ordered validation, not just shuffled grouped CV.
